---
title: "PAg — Professional Agrologist (BC Institute of Agrologists)"
subtitle: "What you need from your UBCO Freshwater Science degree"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## Scaffold placeholder
This page is a scaffold. The authoritative course mapping comes from the
[BCIA UBCO Approved Course List (2025-06-27)](https://www.bcia.com/sites/default/files/docs/resources/UBCO%20Approved%20Courses_June%2027,%202025.pdf).
That PDF is image-based and needs to be OCR'd into a curated YAML before
this page can render the actual list. See `data-raw/02_ocr_bcia_pag_list.R`.
:::

# The designation

The **PAg** (Professional Agrologist) is the senior registration tier of
the BC Institute of Agrologists. To get there, you must first qualify
as an **Articling Agrologist** (AAg) directly from your degree, then
complete BCIA's Articling Program plus two years of agrology-related
work experience.

## Academic eligibility for AAg

Per BCIA, you need:

- A four-year accredited B.Sc. (which Freshwater Science is) with a
  science focus.
- **At least 8 entry-level foundational courses** (100/200 level) from
  the BCIA Academic Worksheet categories.
- **At least 20 courses in agriculture, natural sciences, or
  agriculture / resource economics** that relate to agrology under the
  Agrologists Regulation, 2021.
- **At least 8 senior-level courses** (can be drawn from the 20 above)
  in those same categories.

## Eligible course categories

BCIA recognises courses in:

- Agrology
- Foundational Natural Science
- Mathematics or Statistics
- Economics
- Communications / Writing
- Computer Science

## Your gap-finder

::: {.callout-tip}
## When this is wired up
The interactive planner reads your completed-courses list and the
authoritative BCIA UBCO course mapping, then shows you exactly which
BCIA categories you have covered and which still need fills.
:::

## References

- [New Registrant Requirements (BCIA)](https://www.bcia.com/how-apply/application-requirements/new-registrant-requirements)
- [BCIA UBCO Approved Course List (2025-06-27 PDF)](https://www.bcia.com/sites/default/files/docs/resources/UBCO%20Approved%20Courses_June%2027,%202025.pdf)
- [Agrologists Regulation, 2021](https://www.bclaws.gov.bc.ca/civix/document/id/complete/statreg/47_2021)
